title: Aphananthe aspera
taxonomy: Aphananthe aspera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and is commonly found in woodland and savanna habitats. The genus Aphananthe is a small group of plants that are closely related to the genus Rhus. Aphananthe aspera is a deciduous shrub or small tree that grows up to 10 meters in height. It is characterized by its leathery leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers.
uses: Aphananthe aspera has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
